# GWT Fusion

GWT Fusion provides GWT 2.13 and J2CL-compatible UI components, optional generated icon modules, and a standalone client router.

Start here when using this project with coding agents:

- `docs/agent-guide.md`: Rules and examples for generating GWT Fusion application code.
- `docs/components-index.json`: Canonical machine-readable component and module catalog.
- `docs/components.md`: Human-facing component documentation.
- `docs/events.md`: Listener and event conventions.
- `docs/icons.md`: Icon component usage.
- `docs/icon-modules.md`: Optional icon module usage.
- `docs/router.md`: Standalone router usage.
- `docs/release-readiness.md`: Build, compatibility, test, artifact, and deployment checks.

Core packages:

- `org.gwtfusion.ui`: UI component base types and theme APIs.
- `org.gwtfusion.ui.component.*`: UI components.
- `org.gwtfusion.icons.lucide`: Lucide icon factories.
- `org.gwtfusion.icons.tabler`: Tabler outline icon factories.
- `org.gwtfusion.router`: Standalone router.

Important rules for generated code:

- Use DOM-based `elemental2.dom` APIs with GWT Fusion components.
- Do not use classic GWT widgets such as `com.google.gwt.user.client.ui.Widget`.
- Prefer component factories such as `Button.create(...)`, `Card.create()`, and `Router.create(...)`.
- Prefer enum variants and sizes instead of ad-hoc strings.
- Use semantic listeners such as `onValueChange`, `onCheckedChange`, `onOpenChange`, and `onItemSelect`.
- Do not construct dynamic Tailwind class names such as `"bg-" + color`.
- Use `DirectionManager` for root `dir` handling and logical `START`/`END` sides when placement should follow LTR/RTL.
- Use token-backed themed surfaces such as `bg-card text-card-foreground` and `bg-popover text-popover-foreground`.
